heading: Regions with Only Two Fault Domains
body:
If the selected Azure (Microsoft Azure) region has two fault domains,
Atlas clusters are split across the two fault domains. For example, a
three node replica set cluster would have two nodes deployed to one zone and
the remaining node deployed to the other zone.

![A 3-node replica set deployed across a 2-fault-domain Azure region.](/reference/images/microsoft-azure-2az.svg)

2FD (Two Fault Domain) clusters have a higher chance of loss of
availability in the event of the loss of an zone than 3FD
(Three Fault Domain) clusters. However, where latency or location are a
priority, a region that supports 2FD (Two Fault Domain) clusters
may be preferred.

[^azure-disk-docs]: For detailed documentation on Azure (Microsoft Azure) storage
    options, see [High-performance Premium Storage and managed disks for VMs](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/storage-premium-storage)
